records.ety: [AS. fæstnian; akin to OHG. festinōn. See Fast, a.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Fastened (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Fastening (?).]

defs: To fix firmly; to make fast; to secure, as by a knot, lock, bolt, etc.; as, to fasten a chain to the feet; to fasten a door or window.

To cause to hold together or to something else; to attach or unite firmly; to cause to cleave to something , or to cleave together, by any means; as, to fasten boards together with nails or cords; to fasten anything in our thoughts.
quotes: The words Whig and Tory have been pressed to the service of many successions of parties, with very different ideas fastened to them.

defs: To cause to take close effect; to make to tell; to lay on; as, to fasten a blow.
quotes: If I can fasten but one cup upon him.

defs: To fix one's self; to take firm hold; to clinch; to cling.
quotes: A horse leech will hardly fasten on a fish.
